chapter_title.en: Chapter on Oaths, Vows, and Expiations

text: 4322 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ بَزِيعٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ اَلثَّانِي عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لَهُ رَجُلٌ مَاتَ وَ عَلَيْهِ صَوْمٌ يُصَامُ عَنْهُ أَوْ يُتَصَدَّقُ قَالَ "يُتَصَدَّقُ عَنْهُ فَإِنَّهُ أَفْضَلُ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.4322 - It is narrated from Muhammad ibn Isma'il ibn Bazee' that he said:
I asked Abu Ja'far al-Thani (as) about a man who died and had fasting obligations upon him. Should fasting be done on his behalf, or should charity be given on his behalf?
Imam (as) said: "Charity should be given on his behalf, for it is better."
